---
title: "Clean PLUTO: Core + Land Use + Zoning"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE}
if (!requireNamespace("data.table", quietly = TRUE)) {
  install.packages("data.table")
}
library(data.table)
```

```{r}
# Paths
input_path <- "Datasets/Primary_Land_Use_Tax_Lot_Output_(PLUTO)_20260121.csv"
output_path <- "Datasets/Clean/Primary_Land_Use_Tax_Lot_Output_PLUTO_core_landuse_zoning.csv"

# Core join / spatial keys
core_cols <- c(
  "borough", "borocode", "Tax block", "Tax lot", "BBL",
  "xcoord", "ycoord", "latitude", "longitude",
  "policeprct", "council district", "community board",
  "bct2020", "bctcb2020", "census tract 2010", "tract2010",
  "postcode"
)

# Land use / built environment
landuse_cols <- c(
  "landuse", "bldgclass",
  "lotarea", "bldgarea", "comarea", "resarea", "officearea",
  "retailarea", "garagearea", "strgearea", "factryarea", "otherarea",
  "numbldgs", "numfloors", "unitsres", "unitstotal",
  "yearbuilt", "yearalter1", "yearalter2",
  "builtfar", "residfar", "commfar", "facilfar"
)

# Zoning
zoning_cols <- c(
  "zonedist1", "zonedist2", "zonedist3", "zonedist4",
  "overlay1", "overlay2",
  "spdist1", "spdist2", "spdist3",
  "ltdheight", "splitzone"
)

# Keep geometry for point-in-polygon joins later
keep_cols <- unique(c(core_cols, landuse_cols, zoning_cols, "geom"))

# Read just the needed columns (robust to minor schema changes)
dt <- fread(input_path)
missing_cols <- setdiff(keep_cols, names(dt))
if (length(missing_cols) > 0) {
  warning("Missing columns in source file: ", paste(missing_cols, collapse = ", "))
}

keep_present <- intersect(keep_cols, names(dt))
clean_dt <- dt[, ..keep_present]

# Drop columns that are entirely NA or empty strings
nonempty_cols <- names(clean_dt)[
  vapply(
    clean_dt,
    function(x) {
      if (is.character(x)) {
        any(!is.na(x) & trimws(x) != "")
      } else {
        any(!is.na(x))
      }
    },
    logical(1)
  )
]
clean_dt <- clean_dt[, ..nonempty_cols]

# Write cleaned dataset
fwrite(clean_dt, output_path)
```
